personal.title: Student Developer
personal.headline: Arina Mirzakhani • Student Developer • Computer Programming & Analysis (GBC)
personal.summary: Computer Programming & Analysis student at George Brown College focused on building clear, user-friendly applications. I enjoy turning requirements into working features, documenting decisions, and improving quality through testing and iteration.
personal.bio:
I’m a dependable and detail-oriented student who enjoys solving problems and learning through building. In group work, I focus on clear communication, sharing progress early, and being respectful of deadlines and teammates’ time.

I like environments where expectations are clear and feedback is used to improve. I’m comfortable presenting my work, explaining decisions, and updating documents so others can follow along. I bring a calm, consistent approach to tasks, even when timelines are tight.

Outside of class projects, I’m motivated by work that has a real user in mind. I’m looking for opportunities where I can support a team, keep learning, and take ownership of small-to-medium features end to end.
personal.philosophy:
“We are the facilitators of our own creative evolution.” — Bill Hicks

For me, that means treating software as a craft and a responsibility. I’m motivated by work that makes people’s lives easier—even in small ways—through clarity, reliability, and thoughtful design. I believe my role is to understand the problem, ask better questions, and deliver solutions that are maintainable and respectful of the user’s time.

My past experience has taught me that progress comes from consistent effort: planning before building, testing as I go, and documenting so others can reuse and improve the work. Right now I’m focused on strengthening my fundamentals and building strong project artifacts (requirements, plans, wireframes, and status updates). In the future, I want to grow into a developer who can lead parts of a project, mentor others, and help teams ship high-quality products.
personal.careerSummary: I am a Computer Programming & Analysis student at George Brown College preparing for junior software roles. I build projects by translating requirements into clean, working features and validating them with testing and documentation. I’m comfortable collaborating in teams, using version control, and communicating progress clearly. I stay organized, learn quickly, and adapt when priorities or constraints change. My goal is to join a team where I can contribute to real products while continuing to grow my skills.
marketableSkills: Clear written and verbal communication (status updates, demos, documentation)
Team collaboration (planning, dividing tasks, reviewing work, supporting teammates)
Time management (prioritizing tasks and meeting deadlines)
Problem-solving mindset (break down issues, test assumptions, iterate)
Attention to detail (requirements alignment, consistency, and quality checks)
accomplishments: Delivered multiple academic projects with full development lifecycle artifacts (requirements, design notes, implementation evidence).
Collaborated on team projects using Git-based workflows and clear task ownership.
Prepared structured demo scripts and presentations to explain technical work to non-technical audiences.

capstone.summary: Move-In is a peer-to-peer moving-service platform that helps customers compare vendors, book a service, and track requests in one place.
capstone.vision: Create a simple, trusted marketplace experience for moving services with transparent options, clear pricing signals, and smooth booking.
capstone.requirements: Core flows include browsing/compare vendors, creating a booking request, viewing booking status, and basic account management for customers and vendors.
capstone.implementation: Implemented key customer-side flows (dashboard, booking request, and status views) and supported documentation to track requirements, design, and progress. Linked evidence below (plan, wireframes, status reports, and implementation screenshots).
